mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns do not significantly exceed negative returns during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.4pp, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward returns, or RSAFS and HOUST diverge without impacting equity direction.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity constraints and rising corporate revenues, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index combined with stagnant unemployment creates stagflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3% while UNRATE change < 0.1pp
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL accelerates alongside flat unemployment, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with corporate earnings revisions, establishing a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes show a rank correlation below 0.05 with 60-day forward returns, or earnings revisions decouple from GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls increase exceeding 250,000 signals robust economic momentum that boosts near-term risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where payrolls exceed 250,000, or the signal exhibits no statistical significance after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth alongside near-zero policy rates indicates accommodative monetary conditions supporting manufacturing output, driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns when policy rates are below 0.5%, or industrial production growth fails to translate into equity gains during liquidity traps.
